title: Pav Rolls

overview.description: Soft, buttery Indian bread rolls traditionally served with Pav Bhaji. Light, fluffy and baked together so they pull apart easily.

method: Place the flour, yeast, sugar, salt, butter, milk and water into the bread maker in the order recommended by the manufacturer.
Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface.
Shape each piece into a smooth ball by stretching the surface underneath.
Arrange the balls in a greased square or rectangular baking tin so they are just touching.
Cover and prove for 45–60 minutes until doubled in size.
Bake at 190°C (170°C fan) for 20–25 minutes until golden brown.
Brush generously with melted butter while still hot.
Allow to cool slightly before pulling apart.

learning.did-you-know: The name 'pav' comes from the Portuguese word for bread and reflects the Portuguese influence on western India.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to shape soft bread rolls and bake them together to produce their characteristic pull-apart texture.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Fresh pav transforms homemade Pav Bhaji and the bread maker makes them almost effortless.
chapman-tips: Bake the rolls touching each other to keep the sides soft.
Brushing with butter immediately after baking keeps the crust tender.
The dough also makes excellent burger buns if shaped individually.
Freeze once cooled and refresh for a few minutes in a warm oven.
notes.leftovers: Excellent for breakfast rolls, sandwiches or toasted with butter.
